content.problemStatement:
You are given a directed graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. Let's denote the set of its vertices by VVV, the set of its edges by EEE and an edge from a vertex uuu to a vertex vvv by (u,v)(u, v)(u,v). Then, let's define:


For each u,v‚ąąVu, v \in Vu,v‚ąąV, R(u,v)R(u, v)R(u,v) is true if vvv can be reached from uuu or false otherwise. Specifically, if u=vu = vu=v, it is always true.
For each v‚ąąVv \in Vv‚ąąV, a set of vertices N(v)={u‚ąąV‚ą£R(u,v)‚ąßR(v,u)}N(v) = \{u \in V \mid R(u, v) \wedge R(v, u)\}N(v)={u‚ąąV‚ą£R(u,v)‚ąßR(v,u)}.
For each subset U‚äÜVU \subseteq VU‚äÜV, two sets of edges Out(U)={(u,v)‚ąąE‚ą£u‚ąąU}Out(U) = \{(u,v) \in E \mid u \in U\}Out(U)={(u,v)‚ąąE‚ą£u‚ąąU} and In(U)={(v,u)‚ąąE‚ą£u‚ąąU}In(U) = \{(v,u) \in E \mid u \in U\}In(U)={(v,u)‚ąąE‚ą£u‚ąąU}.
You need to assign a label to each edge in EEE; you may only use labels 111 and 222. The costs of labelling an edge are c1c_1c1‚Äč and c2c_2c2‚Äč for the labels 111 and 222 respectively.

In the resulting graph, QQQ constraints (numbered 111 through QQQ) need to be satisfied. For each valid iii, the iii-th constraint is that the number of edges in a set SiS_iSi‚Äč with the label xix_ixi‚Äč should be between lil_ili‚Äč and rir_iri‚Äč (inclusive); SiS_iSi‚Äč is determined by a given vertex wiw_iwi‚Äč and the type of this constraint tit_iti‚Äč as follows:


ti=1t_i = 1ti‚Äč=1: Si=Out(N(wi))S_i = Out(N(w_i))Si‚Äč=Out(N(wi‚Äč))
ti=2t_i = 2ti‚Äč=2: Si=In(N(wi))S_i = In(N(w_i))Si‚Äč=In(N(wi‚Äč))
ti=3t_i = 3ti‚Äč=3: Si=Out({wi})S_i = Out(\{w_i\})Si‚Äč=Out({wi‚Äč})
ti=4t_i = 4ti‚Äč=4: Si=In({wi})S_i = In(\{w_i\})Si‚Äč=In({wi‚Äč})
Find the smallest cost of labelling all the edges in such a way that these QQQ constraints are satisfied or determine that there is no solution satisfying all constraints.
constraints: 1‚Č§wi‚Č§N1 \le w_i \le N1‚Č§wi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
0‚Č§li‚Č§ri‚Č§M0 \le l_i \le r_i \le M0‚Č§li‚Äč‚Č§ri‚Äč‚Č§M for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 6‚čÖ1046 \cdot 10^46‚čÖ104
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 6‚čÖ1046 \cdot 10^46‚čÖ104
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 6‚čÖ1056 \cdot 10^56‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and QQQ.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting a directed edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v).
The next line contains two space-separated integers c1c_1c1‚Äč and c2c_2c2‚Äč.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains five space-separated integers tit_iti‚Äč, wiw_iwi‚Äč, xix_ixi‚Äč, lil_ili‚Äč and rir_iri‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the smallest cost of labelling the edges or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible to label the edges in such a way that all constraints are satisfied.
